title: jmap.rs: not_created/not_destroyed inline tracing::error + json in single expression is hard to read
description: Multiple places in space_set and message_set use the pattern: { tracing::error!("database error: {e}"); serde_json::json!({"type":"serverFail",...}) } as an inline block expression. This is syntactically valid Rust but visually surprising — the tracing call appears to be part of a value expression. The pattern should be factored out into db_error(e) (which already exists and does exactly this) or at minimum formatted as a let-block. The current style makes code review harder because the tracing call is hidden inside what appears to be a json! macro argument.
